content.full_text:
RAVI MEHTA
CHAIRMAN
FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION
September 15, 1997
Donald K. Wilson, Jr.
Law Offices of Johnnie L. Cochran, Jr.
4929 Wilshire Boulevard, Suite 1010
Los Angeles, California 90010
Re: Your Request for Informal Assistance
Our File No. 1-97-474
Dear Mr. Wilson:
This letter is in response to your request for advice regarding the provisions of the
Political Reform Act (the “Act”)) Because you are not seeking advice regarding a particular
governmental decision, we are treating your request as one for informal assistance.
QUESTION
Does the Political Reform Act restrict or prohibit a California citizen or corporation from.
donating monies to a foreign political party?
CONCLUSION
No. Questions regarding contributions to foreign nationals or political parties are not
within the jurisdiction of the Act.
ANALYSIS
The Act does not contain any provisions which would restrict or prohibit a California
citizen or corporation from donating monies to a foreign political party. Rather, the restrictions
and prohibitions of the Act only apply to California elections. (Section 82022.) Please note that
this advice is limited to the Act and does not provide advice on other restrictions and prohibitions
in federal or state law.
Government Code sections 81000 - 91014. Commission regulations appear at title 2, sections 18109 -
18995, of the California Code of Regulations. Informal assistance does not provide the requestor with the immunity
provided with an opinion or formal written advice. (Section 83114; Regulation 18329(c)(3).)
428 J STREET, SUITE 800, SACRAMENTO, CALIFORNIA 95814
(916) 322-5660


File No. 1-97-474
Page 2
Ifyou have any other questions regarding this matter, please contact me at (916) 322-
5660.
Sincerely,
Steven G. Churchwell
General Counsel
By:
Douglas White
Graduate Assistant, Legal Division
